hypothesis: 3-member ensemble (uniform averaging) on composed #37 baseline. 7 loop-2 runs + loop 1 consistently show single-model over-confidence: output distributions are strongly bimodal, threshold slides but does not soften, perturbations of input/loss/target/weights/regularization either preserve corridor-parking or collapse to the opposite saturation. Ensembling is the program_loop2.md tier 3 lever that targets over-confidence directly: averaging sigmoid outputs across members trained with seeds {42,43,44} produces soft outputs to the extent members disagree on marginal predictions. If the underlying discriminative signal from loop 1's wins (#6/#23/#37) is real, members agree on confident cases and disagree on boundary cases — averaging should preserve discrimination while softening the output distribution and enabling better threshold operation. Uniform averaging satisfies tier 3's no-val-weighting constraint. Label smoothing reverted 0.1→0.0 to isolate ensembling.
change_description: n_ensemble 1 → 3 (uniform sigmoid averaging across seeds 42/43/44); ls 0.1 → 0.0
